text:
Market today: Macro mood is cautious - dollar strength is the real driver to watch. A yen carry trade unwind would spike volatility fast and hit leveraged/tech names hardest. Labor participation at 50-year lows adds recession whispers. Not a day to be adding risk.

Your holdings: Three need attention. CRWD at -72.65% is the open wound - your stop is 590 on a $196 stock, that stop is completely disconnected from reality, fix that now. SOXL at -30.25% is below its $200 stop already - margin means this bleed is costing you extra every day. SPAL is new but already -12.75% and it's a leveraged ETF that decays; stop at $21.38 is close, watch it.

Watch: Dollar/yen - any sharp yen strengthening triggers carry unwind, leveraged ETFs get hit first. Tesla sinking on good delivery numbers is a weird signal; if big-cap tech starts ignoring good news, that's a warning.

Bottom line: Stop-management on CRWD and SOXL is more urgent than any new trade today.
plays: CRWD: Your stop at $590 is a ghost - stock is at $196. Set a real stop near $192.89 (scanner support) or just exit; -72% on margin is a live emergency.
SOXL: Already through your $200 stop. Decide now - cut or reset stop to a level you can defend. Leveraged ETF decay makes holding a loser expensive.
No new buys today - macro carry-trade risk + dollar strength is a headwind for exactly your book; sit tight and protect what you have.
